common.coming_soon: This feature is on the way.

errors.network: Couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
errors.rate_limited: You're checking too quickly. Wait a moment and try again.
errors.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.

home.title: Has your email been exposed in a data breach?
home.subtitle: Enter your email address to find out if it has appeared in any of the breaches we know about.

result.clean: Good news — no breaches were found for that address.
result.found_one: Bad news — your address was found in 1 known breach.
result.found_many: Bad news — your address was found in {{count}} known breaches.
result.next_step_1: Change your password on every site where you reuse the leaked one.
result.next_step_2: Turn on two-factor authentication wherever it is offered.
result.next_step_3: Use a password manager to keep every password unique.

trust.title: How we keep this safe
trust.k_anonymity: Passwords never leave your browser — only the first 5 hex characters of a SHA-1 hash do.
trust.no_sale: We never sell or share your data.
trust.open_design: Our architecture is documented and reviewable.

passwords.title: Has this password been seen in a breach?
passwords.subtitle: Check whether a password has appeared in known breaches.
passwords.privacy_note: Your password never leaves your browser. We send only the first 5 characters of its SHA-1 hash.

result.safe: This password was not found in our list. Still, never reuse a password across sites.
result.pwned_one: This password has been seen 1 time in known breaches. Stop using it everywhere.
result.pwned_many: This password has been seen {{count}} times in known breaches. Stop using it everywhere.

breaches.title: All known breaches
breaches.subtitle: Browse every data breach loaded into Xavfsizmi.

breach.no_description: No description is available for this breach.
breach.not_found_body: We couldn't find a breach with that name.

domains.title: Domain search
domains.subtitle: See every breached email address on a domain you own.
domains.verification_intro: Pick the method that's easiest for you. We accept any one of the three.
domains.method_dns_body: Add a TXT record on your domain containing the verification token we generate.
domains.method_email_body: We send a one-time link to admin@your-domain — open it to confirm.
domains.method_meta_body: Place a <meta> tag with the verification token in the <head> of your homepage.
domains.coming_soon: Domain registration goes live in the next phase.

notifications.title: Notify me of future breaches
notifications.subtitle: Subscribe with your email to get a heads-up if you ever appear in a future breach.
notifications.success: Check your inbox — we sent a confirmation link.
notifications.error: Couldn't subscribe right now. Please try again.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This page explains what data we collect, what we don't, and what your rights are.

what_we_collect.title: What we collect
what_we_collect.body: We collect only the email addresses you submit for lookups, hashed for caching, plus standard request logs.

what_we_dont.title: What we never collect
what_we_dont.body: We never receive your full passwords. We never sell your data. We never share your address with third parties beyond the necessary breach-data provider.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: We use a single cookie to remember your language preference. Analytics cookies are opt-in.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: If you have questions, write to privacy@xavfsizmi.example.

security.title: How we protect your data
security.intro: We were designed to be safe to use even by people who are already worried.

k_anonymity.title: k-anonymity
k_anonymity.body: When you check a password, only the first 5 characters of its SHA-1 hash leave your browser. The matching is done locally against the response.

transport.title: Transport
transport.body: All traffic is served over TLS. We disable old TLS versions and weak ciphers.

storage.title: Storage
storage.body: Subscriber emails are stored hashed. Plaintext addresses are encrypted at rest and only accessed to send notifications.

rate_limits.title: Rate limits
rate_limits.body: Public endpoints are rate-limited per IP and protected with Cloudflare Turnstile so abuse can't drown the service.
